description: Branch Divergence Analysis — credential_file.py and reset_admin.py

src_004.content_summary: write_initial_credentials() — writes admin email+password to {base_dir}/admin_initial_credentials.txt with mode 0600 atomically. No DB calls. Pure file I/O for credential bootstrap.
src_004.risk_rationale: credential_file.py writes credentials to disk but does NOT call init_engine or DB. Mode 0600 is secure. If auth activation proceeds, this file must exist.
src_004.recommended_action: Decide: (A) Import credential_file.py into private/main auth flow, or (B) Implement alternative bootstrap mechanism. File is safe to add without DB changes.

src_005.content_summary: CLI tool to reset admin password. Calls init_engine_from_config(config.database) — PRODUCTION_DB_BLOCKER surface. Modifies DB directly. Also calls write_initial_credentials().
src_005.risk_rationale: reset_admin.py is a CLI tool that calls production DB init. It is NOT called during auth activation. It is a separate operational tool. However, it reveals that the auth system has a DB-backed user store that requires init_engine.
src_005.recommended_action: Do NOT add reset_admin.py to private/main without explicit production_DB authorization. It is a CLI tool for operational use, not part of the activation gate.

full_auth_divergence.description: Complete diff of backend/app/gateway/auth/ between origin/main and private/main

credential_bootstrap_impact.bootstrap_mechanism_on_private: Unknown — setup_status / register endpoint handles first-user creation?

reset_admin_impact.note: reset_admin is a CLI tool, not part of auth activation flow

csrf_middleware_impact.note: CSRF protection is a hardening feature, not required for basic auth activation

activation_impact_review.blocking_gap_description: credential_file.py is needed for credential bootstrap but does not exist on private/main. This directly corresponds to the 'credential_bootstrap' gap identified as BLOCKING in R241-24L.
activation_impact_review.production_risk_description: Without credential_file.py (or equivalent), there is no secure path to create initial admin credentials during auth activation.

pr2645_passive_recheck.ci_triggered: license/cla success only; all other checks not triggered

lane_7_recommendation.reason: Both SRC-004 and SRC-005 are on origin/main only, not on private/main. SRC-004 (credential_file) is a genuine auth activation gap but requires user decision to import. SRC-005 (reset_admin) is high-risk CLI with DB access. SRC-006/SRC-007 (scratch/debug cleanup) are low-risk hygiene items that should be authorized separately.
lane_7_recommendation.requires_user_authorization_for_next: R241-25F requires authorization for file deletion in scratch/ and _debug*.py
lane_7_recommendation.alternative_next: R241-25I — auth activation review (if user wants to address credential_file gap immediately)

next_prompt: Enter R241-25F_HYGIENE_CLEANUP_AUTHORIZATION. R241-25E complete. credential_file.py and reset_admin.py: both exist only on origin/main (missing on private/main). credential_file.py: LOW risk (file write only, no DB). reset_admin.py: HIGH risk (calls init_engine — do NOT import). SRC-006/007 scratch+debug cleanup: low-risk hygiene, needs authorization. R241-25F requires explicit file deletion authorization from user.
